hypothesis: If adaptive, multi-representation diversity constraints (similarity, fragment, and n-gram thresholds) guided by NaviDiv are applied during REINVENT4 reinforcement learning for singlet fission, then the generated molecules will show higher chemical diversity (lower mean pairwise similarity, more clusters, higher unique-fragment percentages, and fewer overrepresented patterns) without reducing the proportion of candidates meeting the ΔE_SF target relative to an unconstrained baseline, because dynamic penalties prevent mode collapse while preserving exploration of property-relevant space.
